title: Hiring people from overseas not in New Zealand :: Immigration New Zealand
content:
## Hiring people from overseas not in New Zealand
Find out what you must do to hire people from overseas in your industry or sector, from specific countries and with particular skills.

## Visa options for hiring people from overseas
There are specific visas that you can use to for bringing people into the country to work for you. You must meet certain requirements to support people applying for these visas and some visas let you hire often, or hire many people at once.
You can also use the 'Support a worker's visa application' tool to find out which visas require employers to be accredited or provide certain information to support the visa application process.
Support a worker's visa application

## General options for hiring workers
- If you are an AEWV accredited employer, migrant workers from overseas can apply for a regular AEWV, or a Global Workforce Seasonal Visa (GWSV) or Peak Seasonal Visa (PSV) – also known as seasonal AEWVs. Accredited Employer Work Visa (AEWV) With an AEWV, a person from overseas can: work in New Zealand for an accredited employer who has offered them at least 30 hours work a week study for up to 3 months in any 12-month period, or do any study required as part of their employment stay in New Zealand for up to 5 years — depending on their job, skill level, how much they are paid and when they applied for their visa. You must pay at least the for the job. You must also show that you cannot find suitable New Zealanders first — unless the job is on the Green List or is paid twice the median wage. Green list roles — jobs we need people for in New Zealand Wage rate requirements for visas AEWV employer accreditation AEWV employer accreditation makes it easier for employers to hire skilled migrant workers where genuine skill or labour shortages exist. There are 3 types of accreditations. Depending on your business and how many people from overseas you want to hire, you can apply for: standard accreditation — for hiring up to 5 people high-volume accreditation — for hiring 6 or more people triangular employer accreditation — if your business places people with for work purposes, and there is no limit to the number of people you can hire. Accredited Employer Work Visa employer information to support an applicant's visa application Overview of employer accreditation and job check Sector agreements and wage exemptions for AEWV workers Accredited Employer Work Visa Seasonal AEWV visas: GWSV and PSV As an AEWV accredited employer, you can hire workers on a GSWV or PSV for seasonal peaks. These visas are streamlined options for employers wanting to hire seasonal workers. They are also a subset of the AEWV, so workers must: meet most of the requirements of the AEWV be on the list of jobs for each visa be genuinely seasonal meet the specific requirements of these visas. To find out more about the seasonal AEWV visas: Global Workforce Seasonal Visa Global Workforce Seasonal Visa jobs Peak Seasonal Visa Peak Seasonal Visa jobs Seasonal visa job durations and hiring months
If you are an AEWV accredited employer, migrant workers from overseas can apply for a regular AEWV, or a Global Workforce Seasonal Visa (GWSV) or Peak Seasonal Visa (PSV) – also known as seasonal AEWVs.

#### Accredited Employer Work Visa (AEWV)
With an AEWV, a person from overseas can:
- work in New Zealand for an accredited employer who has offered them at least 30 hours work a week
- study for up to 3 months in any 12-month period, or do any study required as part of their employment
- stay in New Zealand for up to 5 years — depending on their job, skill level, how much they are paid and when they applied for their visa.
You must pay at least the for the job. You must also show that you cannot find suitable New Zealanders first — unless the job is on the Green List or is paid twice the median wage.
Green list roles — jobs we need people for in New Zealand
Wage rate requirements for visas

#### AEWV employer accreditation
AEWV employer accreditation makes it easier for employers to hire skilled migrant workers where genuine skill or labour shortages exist.
There are 3 types of accreditations. Depending on your business and how many people from overseas you want to hire, you can apply for:
- standard accreditation — for hiring up to 5 people
- high-volume accreditation — for hiring 6 or more people
- triangular employer accreditation — if your business places people with for work purposes, and there is no limit to the number of people you can hire.
Accredited Employer Work Visa employer information to support an applicant's visa application
Overview of employer accreditation and job check
Sector agreements and wage exemptions for AEWV workers
Accredited Employer Work Visa

#### Seasonal AEWV visas: GWSV and PSV
As an AEWV accredited employer, you can hire workers on a GSWV or PSV for seasonal peaks.
These visas are streamlined options for employers wanting to hire seasonal workers. They are also a subset of the AEWV, so workers must:
- meet most of the requirements of the AEWV
- be on the list of jobs for each visa
- be genuinely seasonal
- meet the specific requirements of these visas.
To find out more about the seasonal AEWV visas:
Global Workforce Seasonal Visa
Global Workforce Seasonal Visa jobs
Peak Seasonal Visa jobs
Seasonal visa job durations and hiring months

## Training, work exchanges and country-specific work visas
- If you are an employer or education provider, you can offer practical work experience to overseas students or trainees with this visa, which is needed for their study or training. This can include, but is not limited to, religious trainees, dental or medical trainees and apprentice jockeys. Student and Trainee Work Visa employer information to support an applicant's visa application Student and Trainee Work Visa
If you are an employer or education provider, you can offer practical work experience to overseas students or trainees with this visa, which is needed for their study or training. This can include, but is not limited to, religious trainees, dental or medical trainees and apprentice jockeys.
Student and Trainee Work Visa employer information to support an applicant's visa application
Student and Trainee Work Visa
- You can consider applying for Work Exchange Scheme approval if you want to arrange short-term work opportunities for overseas workers in New Zealand and for New Zealanders overseas, as part of a reciprocal arrangement. Work Exchange Scheme employer information to support an applicant's visa application Work Exchange Scheme Work Visa
You can consider applying for Work Exchange Scheme approval if you want to arrange short-term work opportunities for overseas workers in New Zealand and for New Zealanders overseas, as part of a reciprocal arrangement.

## Visas for specific events, organisations or industries
- Talent (Arts, Culture and Sports) Work Visa If you represent an in the field of art, culture or sports, you can support people from overseas who have exceptional talent in a field of art, culture or sport to come here on the Talent (Arts, Culture and Sports) Work Visa. Talent (Arts, Culture and Sports) Work Visa employer information to support an applicant's visa application Talent (Arts, Culture, Sports) Work Visa

#### Talent (Arts, Culture and Sports) Work Visa
If you represent an in the field of art, culture or sports, you can support people from overseas who have exceptional talent in a field of art, culture or sport to come here on the Talent (Arts, Culture and Sports) Work Visa.
Talent (Arts, Culture and Sports) Work Visa employer information to support an applicant's visa application
Talent (Arts, Culture, Sports) Work Visa
- If you are in the entertainment industry, consider the following options for bringing performers and other entertainment industry workers to New Zealand. Employing overseas entertainers Entertainers Work Visa Before you can hire overseas entertainment industry workers on an Entertainers Work Visa, you must ask the relevant New Zealand entertainment union, guild or professional association for their agreement. Entertainers Work Visa employer information to support an applicant's visa application Entertainers Work Visa Getting entertainment industry support for a work visa application Before you offer a job in the New Zealand entertainment industry to an overseas entertainment industry worker, you must get agreement to support their work visa application from the organisation that represents New Zealand entertainment workers with their skills. We may consult with the New Zealand entertainment industry body to check they support that person's work visa application. Check the list to find out who you need to contact for support. Approved entertainment industry bodies Entertainment Industry Accreditation If you are an entertainment industry employer, you can apply for accreditation to bring overseas entertainment industry workers to New Zealand without first having to ask the relevant New Zealand entertainment union, guild or professional association for their agreement. You can hire multiple workers while you are accredited. Entertainment Industry Accreditation Arts and Music Festival Approval We work with arts and music festival organisers to simplify the visa application process. Performers and direct support workers participating in an ‘approved’ arts or music festival can come to New Zealand as visitors, rather than on work visas. Arts and Music Festival Approval Visitor visas for performers and staff of festivals and entertainment acts Arts and Music Festival Visitor Visa Entertainment Promoters Approval We work with entertainment promoters to simplify the visa application process. Performers and direct support staff of short-term live entertainment acts promoted by an ‘approved promoter’ can enter New Zealand as visitors. Entertainment Promoters Approval Short-term Entertainment Act Visitor Visa
If you are in the entertainment industry, consider the following options for bringing performers and other entertainment industry workers to New Zealand.
Employing overseas entertainers

#### Entertainers Work Visa
Before you can hire overseas entertainment industry workers on an Entertainers Work Visa, you must ask the relevant New Zealand entertainment union, guild or professional association for their agreement.
Entertainers Work Visa employer information to support an applicant's visa application
Entertainers Work Visa
Before you offer a job in the New Zealand entertainment industry to an overseas entertainment industry worker, you must get agreement to support their work visa application from the organisation that represents New Zealand entertainment workers with their skills.
We may consult with the New Zealand entertainment industry body to check they support that person's work visa application.
Check the list to find out who you need to contact for support.
Approved entertainment industry bodies
If you are an entertainment industry employer, you can apply for accreditation to bring overseas entertainment industry workers to New Zealand without first having to ask the relevant New Zealand entertainment union, guild or professional association for their agreement. You can hire multiple workers while you are accredited.
Entertainment Industry Accreditation

#### Arts and Music Festival Approval
We work with arts and music festival organisers to simplify the visa application process. Performers and direct support workers participating in an ‘approved’ arts or music festival can come to New Zealand as visitors, rather than on work visas.
Arts and Music Festival Approval
Visitor visas for performers and staff of festivals and entertainment acts
Arts and Music Festival Visitor Visa

#### Entertainment Promoters Approval
We work with entertainment promoters to simplify the visa application process. Performers and direct support staff of short-term live entertainment acts promoted by an ‘approved promoter’ can enter New Zealand as visitors.
Entertainment Promoters Approval
Short-term Entertainment Act Visitor Visa
- If you are in the fishing industry, you can apply for an Approval in Principle to hire 7 or more crew from overseas to work on a fishing vessel. Approvals in Principle will only be approved if there are not enough New Zealanders available to do the work. Foreign Crew on Fishing Vessels — Approval in Principle You do not need to apply for an Approval in Principle if you want to hire fewer than 6 fishing crew members to work on a single fishing boat. In this case, you can support people to apply for a Fishing Crew Work Visa. Fishing Crew Work Visa employer information to support an applicant's visa application Hiring foreign crew to work on a fishing vessel Fishing Crew Work Visa
If you are in the fishing industry, you can apply for an Approval in Principle to hire 7 or more crew from overseas to work on a fishing vessel. Approvals in Principle will only be approved if there are not enough New Zealanders available to do the work.
Foreign Crew on Fishing Vessels — Approval in Principle
You do not need to apply for an Approval in Principle if you want to hire fewer than 6 fishing crew members to work on a single fishing boat. In this case, you can support people to apply for a Fishing Crew Work Visa.
Fishing Crew Work Visa employer information to support an applicant's visa application
Hiring foreign crew to work on a fishing vessel
Fishing Crew Work Visa
- If you are in the horticulture, agriculture and viticulture industries, consider the following options for bringing people from overseas to New Zealand. Recognised Seasonal Employer (RSE) Scheme Horticulture or viticulture employers can employ overseas workers through the Recognised Seasonal Employer (RSE) Scheme. Recognised Seasonal Employer Limited Visa employer information to support an applicant's visa application Employing workers through the Recognised Seasonal Employer scheme Recognised Seasonal Employer Limited Visa Find employers who have approval to hire workers from overseas under the Recognised Seasonal Employer scheme. Recognised Seasonal Employers list Supplementary Seasonal Employment — Approval in Principle If you need to supplement your New Zealand workforce with workers from overseas to manage seasonal labour demands, you may wish to consider applying for a Supplementary Seasonal Employment (SSE) Approval in Principle. Supplementary Seasonal Employment — Approval in Principle Supplementary Seasonal Employment Work Visa Find employers who can hire temporary visa holders under the Supplementary Seasonal Employer (SSE) Scheme. Supplementary Seasonal Employer list
If you are in the horticulture, agriculture and viticulture industries, consider the following options for bringing people from overseas to New Zealand.

#### Recognised Seasonal Employer (RSE) Scheme
Horticulture or viticulture employers can employ overseas workers through the Recognised Seasonal Employer (RSE) Scheme.

#### Supplementary Seasonal Employment — Approval in Principle
If you need to supplement your New Zealand workforce with workers from overseas to manage seasonal labour demands, you may wish to consider applying for a Supplementary Seasonal Employment (SSE) Approval in Principle.

- Religious Worker Work Visa If you represent a religious organisation that is also a registered charity, you may be able to hire religious workers from overseas. You will need to offer people a job doing religious work and provide sponsorship. Religious Worker Work Visa employer information to support an applicant's visa application Religious Worker Work Visa

#### Religious Worker Work Visa
If you represent a religious organisation that is also a registered charity, you may be able to hire religious workers from overseas. You will need to offer people a job doing religious work and provide sponsorship.
Religious Worker Work Visa employer information to support an applicant's visa application
Religious Worker Work Visa
- Employees of Relocating Business Resident Visa If you are relocating your business to New Zealand, you may be able to bring your key employees with you. For your employees to apply for this visa, they must not be eligible for any other kind of New Zealand resident visa. Employees of Relocating Business Resident Visa employer information to support an applicant's visa application Employees of Relocating Business Resident Visa

#### Employees of Relocating Business Resident Visa
If you are relocating your business to New Zealand, you may be able to bring your key employees with you. For your employees to apply for this visa, they must not be eligible for any other kind of New Zealand resident visa.
Employees of Relocating Business Resident Visa employer information to support an applicant's visa application
Employees of Relocating Business Resident Visa
- You can hire someone from overseas on this visa for a limited time for a specific purpose or event. This includes some business purposes, contract services, sports and entertainment events.
